planets.name: Yeah
planets.summary: Archive cluster around yeah: 23625 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Yeah receipt from 2026-02-08
artifacts.excerpt: At some point when they were playing my 4yo used "blue" to refer to something in between blue and green, likely picking blue because it's her favorite color. The 8yo immediately and cheerfully said "oh! That's actually teal!" And her mom responded that that was inappropriate.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: yeah, too, here, good.

artifacts.title: Yeah receipt from 2026-02-08
artifacts.excerpt: This interaction was frustrating to me because reading the emotions present neither my 4yo and the 8yo did anything that hurt each other, but the low attention parent incorrectly determined harm and told her kid to stop without offering another way to express her perspective.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: yeah, too, here, good.

artifacts.title: Yeah receipt from 2026-02-09
artifacts.excerpt:
Love this shape so much. Such a lifesaver.

When I have a higher dimensional perspective like this shape and someone from lower dimensional space tells me it's actually strictly square, triangle, or circle it becomes so much easier to identify their perspective limitations. https://t.co/PADQfWuzlw
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: yeah, too, here, good.

artifacts.title: Yeah receipt from 2026-02-08
artifacts.excerpt: Now, a better approach might have been to point out the color had both blue and green in it and ask if my 4yo knows a color in that category then suggest teal or turquoise. This might have been good feedback, but this parent was mostly on her phone not paying attention.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: yeah, too, here, good.

artifacts.excerpt:
The pms demon has a useful role in load testing masculinity. 

I used to hate how confused it made me feel early in my relationship, but now it's a time to review the things that bothered her and for me to make course corrections. https://t.co/Xcr770PA3u

artifacts.title: Things receipt from 2026-03-21
artifacts.excerpt:
This is also why bubbles (blocking out signals) can be so dangerous. Things outside of your awareness can change radically leaving you misaligned in proportion to your relative bubble size.

Been so obsessed with Nyquist sampling theorem ever since I came across it. https://t.co/9paTSYyHJi

artifacts.title: Things receipt from 2025-08-31

But, it's worth exploring the pushback for a more integrated perspective. I'd like to formally do this each viral post. I received two categories of criticism. https://t.co/zlbsAJFkRQ
